intro: Asyneuma repandum is a perennial herbaceous plant species within the Campanulaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Asyneuma repandum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly employed in traditional medicine to support health and wellness.
